title: Course Instruction and Delivery
owner_label: Faculty Deans and Heads of Academic Departments
supporting_units: Quality Assurance Directorate (QAD), Office of the School Registrar, MIS, E-Learning Unit, Industry Supervisors
pathway: Approved Course Outline aligned to CLOs→ Lecture delivered on schedule with active methods → Recording of Attendance → Uploading Learning Materials (where applicable) → Monitoring, Evaluation and Feedback.
user_fee: No direct user fees; service is part of tuition and functional fees.
purpose: This standard presents the instruction and delivery of courses.
objectives: To ensure that staff adopt the appropriate instructions and delivery approaches. ii) To ensure that instructions and delivery conform to Competency Based Education and Training (CBET). iii) To conduct course and staff evaluations using SECAT and other approved assessment tools. iv) To ensure that all the topics in the approved course outline are covered as per the timetable. v) To monitor, evaluate and receive feedback on the instructions and delivery of courses for continuous improvement.

title: Student Field Attachment
owner_label: Faculty Deans and Heads of Academic Departments
supporting_units: Quality Assurance Directorate (QAD), Office of the School Registrar, Industry Supervisors
pathway: Submission of FA acceptance letters by Students to FA coordinators (Before end of semester two every Academic Year) →Effective Allocation of Academic Supervisors to students → Submission of Allocation lists and Facilitation for Approval → Receipt of Facilitation and FA Materials → Supervision → Monitoring, Evaluation and Feedback→ Submission of FA Reports.
purpose: This standard presents the administration of Field Attachment.
objectives: To ensure that students obtain practical work experience which they can apply in real workplace settings. (ii) To ensure that administration of FA conforms to Competency Based Education and Training (CBET). (iii) To obtain feedback from employers and field supervisors for continuous improvement of academic programmes.

title: Graduate Research, Supervision and Examination
owner_label: Faculty of Graduate Studies and Research (FGSR)
supporting_units: Quality Assurance Directorate (QAD), Faculty Deans, HODs, Office of the School Registrar, Academic and Administrative Staff, REC Unit.
pathway: Concept development →Supervisor Allocation → Proposal supervision → Plagiarism/REC compliance → Review → Submission of Corrected Proposal→ Data Collection and Analysis→ Dissertation/Thesis/Project writing→ Plagiarism Check → Submission for examination → Oral Examination (Viva Voce/Public Defence) → Recommendations →Submit Corrected Dissertation/Thesis/Project.
purpose: This standard presents the management of graduate research, supervision and examination at MUBS.
objectives: To deliver timely, clear, and actionable feedback on allocation of supervisors, and supervision of proposals and dissertations. (ii) To ensure integrity, fairness and transparency in the supervision and examination process of dissertations/Thesis/projects. (iii) To oversee academic progress and timelines. (iv) To promote intellectual and scholarly development

title: Student Academic Administration and Records Management
supporting_units: Faculties, Departments, QAD, Makerere University
pathway: Admissions → Registration → Timetabling → Examinations → Results Processing → Graduation
user_fee: Application fees, Functional fees, tuition fees as approved by NCHE and MUBS and Graduation fees
purpose: This standard defines the structured, transparent, and efficient academic administration services that support the student lifecycle at MUBS, ensuring compliance with NCHE and Makerere University standards, accuracy of records, and timely delivery of services.
objectives: To ensure fair, transparent, and merit-based student admissions processes. (ii) To ensure accurate student registration and effective management of student records. (iii) To provide efficient, accurate, and clash-free academic timetabling and scheduling services. (iv) To ensure secure, credible, and timely administration of examinations and processing of results. (v) To coordinate orderly graduation processes and timely issuance of academic documents. (vi) To maintain accurate student data, records, and institutional statistics for reporting and decision-making.

title: Career Guidance and outreach service
supporting_units: Faculties, Departments, Public Relations Office, Office of the School Bursar, MUBS Guild
pathway: Career guidance in schools, vocational institutions, and communities → Career talks, exhibitions, mentorship, sensitisation Quality: Adherence to NCHE guidelines, Mak. and MUBS admission standards
user_fee: No user fees charged directly to beneficiaries.
purpose: This standard establishes the minimum level of service expected in the delivery of career guidance programmes to secondary school students, career guidance teachers, school administrators and MUBS students.
objectives: To promote the provision of high-quality career guidance services to students and staff in the MUBS Community, secondary schools, vocational institutions, and other potential clients of MUBS in the community. ii) To ensure effective participation of units, staff, and student ambassadors in delivering interactive and age-appropriate career guidance programmes. iii) To promote equitable access to career guidance services for underserved schools and communities. iv) To support timely admission, registration, and progression of students into higher education programmes. v) To strengthen Makerere University Business School’s commitment to continuous improvement in student success and compliance with national and institutional standards.

title: ICT Infrastructure Management and support
supporting_units: Estates & Works, Strategy & Projects Unit, eLearning Unit
pathway: Assessment → Planning → Configuration → Deployment → Monitoring → Support → Reporting Quality: High-speed connectivity, reliability, cybersecurity, user-centred support, compliance with ICT policies and data protection standards
user_fee: Not Applicable, MUBS management and MUBS Council, professionalism; and methodological rigour Purpose This standard defines the minimum expectations and quality benchmarks for providing digital infrastructure, connectivity, systems maintenance, cybersecurity, and user support at MUBS.
objectives: To provide fast, reliable, and secure internet access for students and staff. ii) To ensure institutional databases and ICT systems are regularly maintained, updated, and protected. iii) To deliver timely, professional, and responsive ICT technical support services. iv) To strengthen monitoring, prevention, and mitigation of cybersecurity threats. v) To ensure efficient operation of MIS in support of academic and administrative functions.

title: HIV/AIDS and Drug Abuse Prevention & Awareness Campaign
supporting_units: Guild Health Ministry, Community Leaders, Security Office, Counselling Unit, Partner Organisations Quality: Timely, confidential, reliable, professional, aligned with national health communication standards
pathway: Health Promotion → Prevention Campaigns → Awareness → Behaviour Change → Reporting Standard Owner: Health Services Centre Supporting Units: Guild Health Ministry, Community Leaders, Security Office, Counselling Unit, Partner Organisations Quality: Timely, confidential, reliable, professional, aligned with national health communication standards
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for planning, implementing, and monitoring health campaigns at MUBS.
objectives: To provide students and staff with accurate, timely, and confidential information on HIV/AIDS and drug abuse. ii) To engage qualified health practitioners and industry experts in the professional facilitation of awareness campaigns. iii) To promote prevention, early detection, and responsible health-seeking behaviours through awareness programmes. iv) To ensure health campaigns are accessible through diverse channels, including physical engagements and digital platforms. v) To contribute to national health priorities and enhance the overall wellbeing of students and staff.
pillar: Research, Innovation, Employability and Community Engagement

title: Student Welfare, Leadership, Sports and Health Services
supporting_units: Office of the School Registrar, Health Centre, Human Resource, Directorate, Sports Office, School Bursar’s Office
pathway: Application/Request → Verification → Approval → Service Delivery → Monitoring → Reporting → Learning Quality: Transparency; Timeliness; Compliance with MUBS policies, NCHE guidelines, Government regulations, Disability and Sports Policies; Professionalism; Inclusivity
user_fee: Free Purpose The Student Welfare, Leadership and Sports standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for administering student welfare services at MUBS. Objective: i) To ensure the timely, accurate, and secure disbursement of living-out allowances for food and accommodation to registered government-sponsored students. ii) To facilitate transparent, democratic, and constitutionally compliant student leadership structures through the systematic coordination, budgeting, and execution of annual Guild elections and cabinet selections. iii) To cultivate athletic excellence, physical wellness, and competitive sports development across all approved university programs by deploying qualified coaches and maintaining strict compliance with National Council of Sports standards. iv) To champion diversity and equity in MUBS sports by actively collaborating with the Disability Resource and Learning Centre to maximize the participation rate of students with disabilities in institutional tournaments and national leagues. v) To enforce rigorous administrative controls and document verification protocols to validate student registration, financial eligibility, and academic progress before granting access to welfare allowances or leadership positions. =====PAGE 99===== 85
objectives: To ensure the timely, accurate, and secure disbursement of living-out allowances for food and accommodation to registered government-sponsored students. ii) To facilitate transparent, democratic, and constitutionally compliant student leadership structures through the systematic coordination, budgeting, and execution of annual Guild elections and cabinet selections. iii) To cultivate athletic excellence, physical wellness, and competitive sports development across all approved university programs by deploying qualified coaches and maintaining strict compliance with National Council of Sports standards. iv) To champion diversity and equity in MUBS sports by actively collaborating with the Disability Resource and Learning Centre to maximize the participation rate of students with disabilities in institutional tournaments and national leagues. v) To enforce rigorous administrative controls and document verification protocols to validate student registration, financial eligibility, and academic progress before granting access to welfare allowances or leadership positions.

title: Disability Inclusion and Resource Mobilization
supporting_units: Human Resource Directorate, Office of the School Bursar, Office of the Dean of Students, Office of the School Registrar’s Office, Estates and Facilities Directorate, ICT Directorate, External Disabled Persons Organizations (DPOs)
pathway: Needs Assessment → Stakeholder Engagement → Intervention Design → Resource Preparation → Implementation → Monitoring & Evaluation Quality: 100% compliance with the Uganda Persons with Disabilities Act (2020), MUBS Strategic Plan, building accessibility regulations, and global universal design frameworks
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, operational processes, and quality benchmarks for delivering disability advocacy, inclusive training, and strategic resource sourcing initiatives at MUBS.
objectives: To foster an empathetic, inclusive, and non-discriminatory institutional culture across all MUBS administrative and academic departments. ii) To equip academic faculty with functional capabilities in inclusive teaching, testing, and assessment methodologies for learners with diverse functional needs. iii) To bridge communication gaps across campuses by delivering structured Ugandan Sign Language training workshops to frontline staff and student leaders. iv) To systematically map, track, and target global and domestic donor funding opportunities matching the school's inclusive development roadmap. v) To build strategic consortia and joint research partnerships with grant-experienced organizations and international academia. vi) To secure external development capital specifically targeted at high-tech assistive technologies, physical infrastructure retrofits, and digital portal accessibility upgrades.

title: Medical Support and Counselling Services
supporting_units: Dean of Students’ Office, Human Resource Directorate, Disability Resource & Learning Centre
pathway: Triage → Consultation → Diagnosis → Treatment → Counselling/Referral → Follow-Up → Reporting Quality: Timely, inclusive, non-discriminatory, confidential, aligned with MUBS HR Manual and national health standards
purpose: The Medical Support and Counselling Services Standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for delivering timely, professional, and inclusive health and psychosocial services to students and staff at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that all patients receive prompt triage, consultation, diagnosis, treatment, and follow-up care. ii) To provide accessible, confidential, and responsive counselling services addressing mental, social, and emotional needs. iii) To ensure that emergency cases are promptly prioritised, managed, and reported to the relevant authorities. iv) To promote holistic wellbeing that supports academic success and staff productivity through integrated medical and counselling services. v) To maintain accurate medical and counselling records, referrals, and reports to ensure continuity of care, compliance, and effective institutional planning.

matrix_rows.process_text: Conduct triage and initial assessment in 10 minutes→ Provide doctor’s consultation in 15 minutes → Carry out laboratory tests and/or scans in 20 minutes → Complete diagnosis and initiate treatment plan in10 minutes → Recommend counselling or make a referral as required → Provide nursing care and support as required → Formulate clinical referral notes for tertiary medical facilities when required → Dispense prescribed medication at the pharmacy 5 minutes Time: Triage finalized within 10 minutes; routine clinician consultation initiated within 30 minutes of triage; routine lab diagnostics completed within 45 minutes; pharmacy dispensing completed within 10 minutes of electronic prescription receipt. Accessibility: Physical walk-in access at the School Health Services Centre; emergency ambulance services dispatched via dedicated campus lines.

title: Staff Medical, Welfare and Social Benefits Administration Standard Key Service Area: Staff Medical Care, Welfare Support, Social Benefits, Compensation, Retirement Benefits
supporting_units: Health Services Centre, Office of the School Bursar, Registrar’s Office, Insurance Providers, MURBS/NSSF
pathway: Enrolment → Verification → Service Delivery → Claims Processing → Reporting → Review Quality: Compliance with MUBS HR Manual (2009, amended 2024), Workers Compensation Act, NSSF Act, URBRA guidelines, confidentiality, timeliness, professionalism
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for delivering medical assistance, welfare support, compensation, and statutory benefits to staff at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that staff and their registered dependents receive timely, compliant, and quality medical care assistance. ii) To ensure that all work-related injuries and illnesses are reported, assessed, and compensated in accordance with the Workers’ Compensation Act. iii) To provide confidential, professional, and accessible counselling services to staff and their families. iv) To ensure the prompt and compassionate processing of death benefits to support bereaved families. v) To administer wedding (nuptial) support fairly and in accordance with the Human Resource Manual. vi) To ensure accurate and timely processing of tuition waiver and related educational benefits for staff biological children. vii) To ensure accurate remittance and annual reporting of retirement benefits and statutory contributions (MURBS/NSSF).

title: Security and Safety Management
supporting_units: Estates & Works, Dean of Students’ Office, Health Services Centre, ICT Unit Quality: Safe campus environment; timely response; compliance with institutional security protocols; professionalism
pathway: Campus Security → Patrols → Emergency Response → Incident Management → Reporting Standard Owner: Security Section Supporting Units: Estates & Works, Dean of Students’ Office, Health Services Centre, ICT Unit Quality: Safe campus environment; timely response; compliance with institutional security protocols; professionalism
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for ensuring a safe, secure, and supportive environment for students, staff, and visitors at MUBS.
objectives: To conduct regular campus patrols to deter crime and enhance institutional safety. ii) To ensure prompt, professional, and effective response to all emergency incidents. iii) To manage lost and found items securely, transparently, and efficiently. iv) To provide security services that support a safe environment for teaching, learning, research, and campus life.

title: Scholarships administration
supporting_units: Dean of students' office, Health centre, Human Resource Directorate
pathway: Advertise→ Compile – verify – Recommend→ short list →Interview → Approvals → Award Quality: Transparency, Compliance with sponsor, and NCHE guidelines, MUBS first class policy, disability policy and sports scholarship policy
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for managing all scholarship awards at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that all scholarship processes are transparent, fair, and compliant with institutional, national, and sponsor guidelines. ii) To identify, verify, and award scholarships to eligible students, including academic achievers, persons with disabilities, and sports persons, through a structured and accountable process. iii) To ensure that scholarship decisions are made in accordance with approved governance structures. iv) To ensure timely, accessible, and consistent communication with scholarship applicants and beneficiaries across all platforms. v) To ensure prompt application of scholarship funds to student accounts upon receipt of approved sponsor lists or funds.

matrix_rows.service_description: Inputs Administration and Award of Institutional and Sponsored Scholarships
performance_indicators: No. of students successfully placed and maintained on scholarship programs.
% of tuition and functional fees accurately disbursed to student portal accounts within approved timelines.
Prop. of student awards distributed equitably across the distinct scholarship categories
Rate of compliance with specific donor covenants, statutory guidelines, and institutional policies.
Turnaround time for processing and verifying applications from the closing date to the final award letter issuance.

title: Legal Services and Institutional Compliance
supporting_units: Office of the Principal, Office of the School Secretary, Human Resource Directorate, Office of the School Registrar’s Office, All Units Quality: Legally compliant, risk-free, accurate, confidential, timely, aligned with MUBS policies and national laws
pathway: Request → Registration → Legal Analysis → Consultation → Drafting/Review → Approval → Implementation → Reporting Standard Owner: Directorate of Legal Affairs Supporting Units: Office of the Principal, Office of the School Secretary, Human Resource Directorate, Office of the School Registrar’s Office, All Units Quality: Legally compliant, risk-free, accurate, confidential, timely, aligned with MUBS policies and national laws
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for providing legal support, institutional compliance, governance advisory, and dispute resolution services at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that all contracts, agreements, and Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) are legally compliant, risk-aware, and aligned with institutional policies. ii) To provide accurate, evidence-based, confidential, and timely legal opinions. iii) To manage litigation processes professionally, ensuring timely filings, representation, and resolution of cases. iv) To ensure that institutional policies are aligned with national laws, regulatory frameworks, and applicable best practices. v) To provide effective legal guidance and support to Council and Committee meetings. vi) To oversee staff and student disciplinary processes in a fair, transparent, and timely manner. vii) To strengthen legal awareness through structured training and sensitisation programmes. viii) To ensure secure storage, proper indexing, and easy retrieval of legal records.
pillar: Human Capital, Governance, and Institutional Sustainability

title: Staff Performance Management and Development
supporting_units: Office of the School Secretary, All Units, Top Management, Appointments Board, MUBS council Quality: Compliance with MUBS HR Manual (2009, amended 2024), Employment Act, Public Service Standards; fairness, transparency, timeliness, professionalism
pathway: Onboarding → Target Setting → Performance Monitoring → Appraisal → Workforce Assessment → Leave Management → Staff Development → Promotion Standard Owner: Human Resource Directorate Supporting Units: Office of the School Secretary, All Units, Top Management, Appointments Board, MUBS council Quality: Compliance with MUBS HR Manual (2009, amended 2024), Employment Act, Public Service Standards; fairness, transparency, timeliness, professionalism
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for managing staff performance, growth, and progression at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that all newly recruited and appointed staff are effectively onboarded and integrated into their respective roles. ii) To establish, monitor, and appraise performance targets in a structured, transparent, and objective manner. iii) To conduct fair and timely workforce assessments to support promotions, confirmations, and career progression. iv) To manage staff, leave, in a compliant, accurate, and efficient manner. v) To align staff development initiatives with institutional needs, quality assurance standards, and individual career aspirations. vi) To ensure that staff promotions are conducted through merit-based, transparent, and timely procedures.
pillar: Human Capital, Governance, and Institutional Sustainability

matrix_rows.service_description: Staff Onboarding of new staff
performance_indicators: % of newly recruited staff successfully on boarded within the stipulated timeframe.
Completion rate of induction/orientation programmes for new staff.

matrix_rows.quality_standard: Conforms Human Resource Manual 2009 (As Ammended In 2024) https://hrms.mubs.ac.ug/hr-docs/view2/1
matrix_rows.process_text: HR Directorate (HRD) interfaces with the new hire, collects primary biodata, and issues critical HR files (Within 5 working days of contract acceptance) →Notify the respective Head of Department (HOD) of the new hire's reporting date (Within 5 working days) → Formal introduction to the HOD, department team, and assignment of role expectations/academic workflows (Within 5 working days) →HOD submits written confirmation of the employee's Assumption of Duty to HRD (Within 5 working days) →Set up official corporate email, build the HRMS portal profile, and capture banking/tax data for payroll entry (Within 5 working days) → HOD assesses role clarity, institutional cultural fit, and early training needs (Within 3 months) →Conduct initial probation performance review and deliver feedback to the hire (Within 3 months) →Submit a consolidated onboarding cycle report to Top Newly Recruited Academic and Administrative Staff -Issuance and formal acceptance of a valid MUBS employment contract. -Physical reporting for duty at the assigned station. Physical: Face-to-face departmental introductions, physical campus tours, one-on-one mentor pairing, and interactive peer-networking workshops. Online/Digital: Digital provisioning of IT accounts, mandatory virtual compliance modules on the intranet, and online employee profile registration. -MUBS HR Manual 2009 (As amended). -Standard onboarding checklist and welcome documentation pack. -Secure HRMS portal login credentials. -Departmental role profiles and KPI template =====PAGE 159===== 145 Management (Within 3 months post-cycle). Time: Step-by-step entry loops finalized within 5 working days; full probationary orientation completed within 3 months.

matrix_rows.quality_standard: 100% compliance with Public Finance Management Act https://www.finance.go.ug/sites/default/files/2023-10/Uganda%20Public%20Finance%20Management%20Act%202015.pdf International Standards for the Professional Practice of Internal Auditing (IIA Standards), and the Local Governments Financial and Accounting Regulations.
matrix_rows.process_text: Draft the risk-based annual audit workplan aligned to the approved MUBS strategic plan and budget (Within 4 weeks) →Forward the annual workplan to the Audit and Risk Management Committee of Council (2 weeks prior to meeting) →Submit the approved Annual Workplan to the Internal Auditor General, copying the Accounting Officer (By April) →Plan quarterly engagements defining scope (Financial, compliance, value for money, operational, environmental, performance) and allocate field auditors (3 days) →Issue formal information requests to target auditees (1 day) →Schedule and conduct operational entry meetings with auditees (Within 2 weeks) →Gather, extract, and compile financial and administrative records →Conduct comprehensive field testing and compile formal audit findings →Submit draft reports with remedial recommendations to Management for management responses (By the 10th day of the subsequent month post-quarter) →Aggregate management responses into the final report and MUBS Council, Top Management, Internal Auditor General, Auditor General, PPDA, IGG, MoES, MoFPED, and Parliament Inclusion within the approved annual risk-based audit workplan. -Formally approved Council or ministerial accountability mandate. Physical: In-person entry/exit meetings, physical asset verification, cash counts, warehouses/stores structural inspections, face-to-face interviews, and hardcopy voucher tracing. Online/Digital: Remote audit portal reviews, electronic ledger transaction analytics, digital extraction of system logs, and secure virtual presentation of findings to Council committees. -Approved Annual Audit Workplan. - Access to institutional financial registries (IFMIS/Navision). - Prior audit reports and pending tracking logs. - National financial regulation manuals. - Internal Audit department technical human resources. =====PAGE 167===== 153 present to Council (Within 15 days) →Disseminate approved Quarterly Audit Reports to the Internal Auditor General, Ministry of Education, and MoFPED (Within 30 days post-quarter) →Compile and submit the Annual Consolidated Internal Audit Report to the Internal Auditor General (Within 30 days post-FY) →Conduct quarterly audit follow-ups to verify implementation status of past recommendations and update Council →Coordinate end-of-year exit parameters with the Auditor General/External Auditors. Time: Workplans finalised by April; quarterly drops completed within 30 days post-quarter; annual consolidations processed within 30 days of FY close.

matrix_rows.frequency: Executed continuously; reported Quarterly and Annually. =====PAGE 168===== 154 F. Legal Services and Institutional Compliance Standard

title: Procurement, Contract Management and Disposal
supporting_units: Office of the School Bursar, Internal Audit, User Departments, Contracts Committee, PPDA Quality: Compliance with PPDA Act & Regulations, transparency, fairness, timeliness, value for money
pathway: Procurement Planning → Solicitation → Evaluation → Contract Award → Contract Management → Supplier Evaluation → Disposal → Records Management Standard Owner: Procurement and Disposal Unit (PDU) Supporting Units: Office of the School Bursar, Internal Audit, User Departments, Contracts Committee, PPDA Quality: Compliance with PPDA Act & Regulations, transparency, fairness, timeliness, value for money
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for planning, soliciting, evaluating, awarding, managing, and closing procurement and disposal activities at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that all procurement activities comply with the PPDA Act, related regulations, and institutional policies. ii) To promote timely and participatory procurement planning aligned with approved budgets and institutional priorities. iii) To ensure transparency, fairness, and competitiveness in bid solicitation, evaluation, and contract award processes. iv) To strengthen contract management in order to ensure timely delivery, quality assurance, and value for money. v) To establish objective supplier performance evaluation mechanisms to support informed future procurement decisions. vi) To ensure transparent, timely, and compliant disposal of institutional assets. vii) To maintain secure, well-indexed, and easily retrievable procurement records for audit and compliance purposes.
pillar: Human Capital, Governance, and Institutional Sustainability

title: Strategic Planning, Work Planning and Monitoring & Evaluation
supporting_units: Office of the School Bursar, Procurement & Disposal Unit (PDU), Internal Audit, All Faculties, Departments & Campuses Quality: 100% alignment with MUBS Strategic Plan, NDP, MOFPED guidelines, PPDA laws, and institutional policies
pathway: Strategic Planning → Workplan Consolidation → Budget Alignment → Procurement Planning → Implementation → Monitoring & Evaluation → Reporting Standard Owner: Strategy & Projects Unit Supporting Units: Office of the School Bursar, Procurement & Disposal Unit (PDU), Internal Audit, All Faculties, Departments & Campuses Quality: 100% alignment with MUBS Strategic Plan, NDP, MOFPED guidelines, PPDA laws, and institutional policies
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for preparing institutional plans, aligning budgets, extracting procurement plans, and monitoring implementation at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that all plans and budgets are aligned with the MUBS Strategic Plan. ii) To coordinate the consolidation, review, and timely approval of annual and quarterly work plans. iii) To facilitate the preparation and submission of procurement plans derived from approved work plans to the Procurement and Disposal Unit (PDU) for implementation. iv) To strengthen monitoring and evaluation processes for tracking performance, assessing results, and supporting evidence-based decision-making. v) To ensure timely submission of reports to Management and relevant committees in order to enhance accountability and continuous improvement.
pillar: Human Capital, Governance, and Institutional Sustainability

matrix_rows.quality_standard: Compliance with the Universities And Other Tertiary Institutions Act Chapter 262 https://media.ulii.org/media/legislation/128221/source_file/ebbb21906638186d/2001-7.pdf
matrix_rows.process_text: Draft and secure Council approval for the formulation roadmap and budget→ Conduct baseline performance reviews and comprehensive institutional SWOT/PESTLE environmental scans → Administer stakeholder consultation workshops across all MUBS constituencies → Draft core strategic pillars, institutional objectives, and macro–Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) → Align the strategic plan costing matrix with macro-economic realities and institutional budget ceilings → Validate the draft plan with Top Management, Council, and external certification authorities (NPA) → Publish, distribute, and officially launch the approved Strategic Plan. Time: Master roadmap executed over a strict 6-month period; finalized plan submitted to Council at least 3 months prior to the expiry of the current active plan.
matrix_rows.coverage: MUBS Main Campus and all Regional Campuses.
matrix_rows.frequency: Executed once every 5 years (at the commencement of a new strategic cycle). MUBS Top Management, Council, Staff, Students, Ministry of Education and Sports, NPA, and Public Partners -Expiry or scheduled sunsetting of the existing 5-year Institutional Strategic Plan. -Executive directive and resource allocation from the MUBS Council. Physical: In-person stakeholder brainstorming workshops, strategic prioritization retreats, technical drafting sessions, and physical presentations to Top Management and Council. Online: Intranet feedback collection portals, virtual planning webinars, and digital publishing of approved strategic guidelines. -National Development Plan (NDP) directives. -NPA strategic planning templates and manuals. -Baseline institutional performance data. -Top Management technical briefs. -Dedicated planning steering committee human resources. =====PAGE 144===== 130

purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for managing financial transactions, budgeting, reporting, payroll, compliance, and revenue collection at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure accurate and timely tuition billing and receipting in alignment with student registration records. ii) To facilitate budget preparation and execution in accordance with approved timelines, institutional priorities, and national guidelines. iii) To ensure the preparation and timely submission of accurate and compliant financial reports. iv) To process payroll accurately, confidentially, and within the stipulated payment timelines. v) To address audit and compliance queries promptly, transparently, and effectively. vi) To ensure that all collected revenue is properly accounted for, reconciled, and reported.
pillar: Human Capital, Governance, and Institutional Sustainability

title: Governance and Secretariat Services
supporting_units: Council Secretariat, Human Resource Directorate, Directorate of Planning, Office of the School Registrar’s Office, All Units, Top Management
pathway: Request → Registration → Agenda Preparation → Consultation → Meeting Facilitation → Documentation → Communication → Implementation Tracking → Reporting Quality: Accurate, complete, compliant with the MUBS Charter, timely, transparent, accessible, aligned with institutional policies and national frameworks
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for providing council secretariat support, governance advisory, and institutional compliance services at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that Council and Committee meetings are properly convened, guided, and documented in accordance with the MUBS Charter and statutory requirements. ii) To accurately record, communicate, and track the implementation of Council and Committee resolutions. iii) To provide timely and quality secretariat services, including meeting notices, board packs, minutes, and action reports. iv) To ensure that institutional policies and decisions are aligned with established governance frameworks and best practices. v) To manage staff and student disciplinary processes in a fair, transparent, and timely manner. vi) To strengthen governance awareness through structured training, sensitisation, and retreat programmes. vii) To ensure secure storage, proper indexing, and easy retrieval of Council records, minutes, and compliance reports.
pillar: Human Capital, Governance, and Institutional Sustainability

matrix_rows.service_description: Table 3:30: Governance and Secretariat Services Standard Output / Service Performance Indicator Standard (Quality, Time, Accessibility, Coverage, Frequenc Provision of Council Secretariat & Governance Services
performance_indicators: Total number of statutory Council and specialized committee meetings successfully organized and supported.
% of comprehensive boardroom agenda packs compiled and securely dispatched within statutory notice periods.
Turnaround time for drafting, validating, and circulating master meeting minutes to members.
matrix_rows.quality_standard: 100% compliance with the MUBS Charter, Universities And Other Tertiary Institutions Act Chapter 262 https://media.ulii.org/media/legislation/128221/source_file/ebbb21906638186d/2001-7.pdf, and approved Council Rules of Procedure.
matrix_rows.process_text: Develop, secure approval for, and publish the Annual Governance Meetings Almanac (At start of FY) →Source, screen, and compile strategic business papers and executive briefs from institutional cost-centres (Ongoing over 3 months) →Edit, index, and securely distribute official meeting notices and comprehensive board packs to members (Exactly 7 calendar days prior to the meeting) →Provide legal and procedural guidance during boardroom sessions and maintain live minutes logs →Formulate, polish, and circulate the first draft of meeting minutes to members for feedback (Within 7 calendar days post-meeting) →Capture and integrate amendments or corrections submitted by members (Within 5 working days of minutes receipt) →Archive signed master minutes into the central institutional registry. Time: Board packs dispatched 7 days prior to sessions; draft minutes circulated within 7 days post-session; member feedback captured within 5 days.
matrix_rows.coverage: Full Main Council and all its statutory sub-committees (e.g., Finance, Appointments, Audit, Development).
matrix_rows.frequency: Conducted continuously; formal statutory sessions held At least 4 times a Year (Quarterly) per committee. Online/Digital: Deployment of specialized encrypted board portal software, digital distribution of board packs, and hosting secure virtual/hybrid governance webinars. -Universities and Other Tertiary Institutions Act (UOTIA) 2001. - Approved MUBS Charter and board rules. -Strategic performance reports from internal university units. -Secure web-based Board Management Portals. -Dedicated Council Secretariat human resources. =====PAGE 174===== 160 Monitoring and Tracking of Council Resolutions Implementation i)Number of official board resolutions formally extracted, signed, and communicated to executive management for action. ii) % of approved Council directives successfully implemented by Management within agreed timelines. iii) Timeliness of compiling and presenting the master Council Resolutions Action Report.

matrix_rows.quality_standard: Absoluteness, accuracy and completeness and traceability in compliance with the MUBS Council Charter 2024
matrix_rows.process_text: Extract and format individual binding resolutions from the raw confirmed minutes of Council sessions (Within 24 hours of meeting closure) → Issue formal Action Directives signed by the Chairperson/Secretary to the Principal to activate executive implementation (Within 1 working day) → Deploy tracking matrices to monitor ongoing operational execution across user units → Collect documentary evidence and field-test compliance claims submitted by Management →Aggregate findings to compile a comprehensive, data-backed Council Resolutions Action Report tracking implementation milestones →Present the Action Report to the Main Council at its subsequent statutory session to highlight progress and performance gaps. Time: Resolutions extracted and issued to the Principal within 24 hours; cumulative Action Reports updated every 3 months for the subsequent Council drop.

Online/Digital: Maintenance of an automated digital Council Resolutions Tracker Spreadsheet on the intranet, and electronic collection of milestone data from departments. -Confirmed master minutes of Council and committee sessions. -Standardized Resolution Extraction Templates. -Departmental implementation progress logs. -Specialized tracking/project management dashboard software. -Dedicated Quality Assurance and Audit tracking units. =====PAGE 175===== 161 CONCLUSION The MUBS SDSs mark a major step in strengthening the quality, consistency, and accountability of service delivery across MUBS. They reflect the commitment of MUBS to providing services that are timely, professional, transparent, and responsive to the needs of students, staff, partners, and other stakeholders. These Standards represent a shared commitment to uphold the values and principles that define MUBS as an institution of excellence. They provide a clear understanding of the level of service stakeholders should expect and the responsibility of every member of the MUBS community in achieving those standards. The successful implementation of the SDSs will depend on the collective efforts and teamwork of management, staff, students, and stakeholders working together towards a common goal of improving institutional performance and stakeholder satisfaction. Through its core values of professionalism, teamwork, innovation, responsiveness, and effective use of technology, MUBS commits to strengthening its culture of quality service delivery and continuous improvement. As MUBS moves forward in implementing these Standards, the institution reaffirms its commitment to academic excellence, effective governance, innovation, and stakeholder-centered service delivery. The SDSs therefore provide a framework for service delivery and a foundation for building a stronger, more responsive, and future-oriented institution.

title: International Student Services and Internationalisation
supporting_units: Office of the School Registrar, School Bursar’s Office, MIS, eLearning
pathway: Admission/Engagement → Pre-arrival Preparation → Immigration & Travel Coordination → Arrival & Reception → Registration → Orientation → Integration → Continuous Support → Monitoring → Reporting → Learning
user_fee: Free Purpose This standard defines the minimum service expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for attracting, onboarding, integrating, and supporting international students, visiting scholars, and other international academic participants at MUBS.
objectives: To attract, support admission, onboarding, and registration of international students within prescribed MUBS timelines. ii) To facilitate full compliance with immigration, visa, and travel requirements for international students, visiting scholars, and academic staff through coordinated institutional support and liaison with relevant government authorities. iii) To provide structured pre-arrival, arrival, and post-arrival support services, including accommodation facilitation, documentation verification, registration, and issuance of institutional identification and access credentials. iv) To promote effective academic, social, and cultural integration of international students into the MUBS community through structured orientation programmes, mentorship, and engagement in campus life. v) To ensure continuous monitoring and support of international students’ academic progress, welfare, and adjustment, including timely referral of identified challenges to relevant support units. vi) To strengthen MUBS Internationalisation by facilitating student and staff mobility programmes, international partnerships, exchange programmes, visiting scholar engagement, and global academic collaboration.

matrix_rows.service_description: Table 3:23: International Student Services and Internationalisation Standard Output / Service Performance Indicator Standard (Quality, Time, Accessibility, Coverage, Process, Frequenc International Onboarding and transition No of international students onboarded and registered on time. Satisfaction rate of international students Percentage of international students successfully integrated into MUBS social and support networks within their first semester.
matrix_rows.quality_standard: 100% error-free rate on all government immigration portal submissions.
matrix_rows.process_text: Compile information on admitted international students within 3 working days of receiving admission lists → Review and verify beneficiary documentation for immigration support within 2 working days → Coordinate visa and immigration support with the relevant government offices → Facilitate accommodation arrangements and other essential pre-arrival requirements, where applicable, within 1 week → Coordinate arrival logistics with relevant School offices and provide reception and transition support upon arrival → Verify original travel and immigration documents upon arrival → Coordinate issuance of MUBS identification cards and other institutional documentation within 2 working days of registration → Conduct orientation on MUBS policies, academic systems, student support services, and campus life within 4 working days of arrival→ Monitor students throughout the transition period and promptly identify and refer academic, financial, immigration, welfare, or psychosocial concerns to the appropriate support units → Transition students into the standard MUBS student support systems and submit periodic onboarding reports to Management.

title: Research Grants Support, and Ethics, Clearance Strategic Pillar: Quality Assurance, Research Productivity & Institutional Reputation
owner_label: Faculty of Graduate Studies and Research
supporting_units: Faculties, Graduate School, Ethics Committee, ICT Unit, Finance, Library Quality: Accurate, ethical, timely, compliant with NCHE, Makerere University QA Guidelines, MUBS QA Policy
pathway: Grants Support → Ethics Clearance → Monitoring & Reporting Standard Owner: Faculty of Graduate Studies and Research Supporting Units: Faculties, Graduate School, Ethics Committee, ICT Unit, Finance, Library Quality: Accurate, ethical, timely, compliant with NCHE, Makerere University QA Guidelines, MUBS QA Policy
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for supporting research activities, ensuring ethical compliance, and monitoring graduate supervision at MUBS.
objectives: To provide timely and accurate support to staff and students in the identification, preparation, and submission of research grant proposals. ii) To ensure that all research involving human participants, data, or sensitive information undergoes ethical review and approval within established timelines. iii) To ensure compliance of research activities with national and institutional research ethics guidelines and frameworks. iv) To enhance institutional visibility, accreditation, and knowledge generation through quality research outputs.

title: Industry Partnership
supporting_units: Training Team, Outreach Centres, School Secretary’s Office, Partner Organisations, Faculties & Departments
pathway: Course Design → Promotion & Registration → Training Delivery → Assessment & Certification → Partnerships & MoUs → Monitoring & Reporting Purpose This standard defines the structured, quality-assured, and inclusive processes through which the ICT Centre delivers digital skills training, customised organisational ICT programmes, community outreach initiatives, student and staff bootcamps, and industry partnerships for internships and apprenticeships.
objectives: To equip learners with industry-relevant digital skills through structured short courses, tailored training, and bootcamps. ii) To expand digital inclusion through sponsored community outreach programmes targeting vulnerable and marginalised groups. iii) To strengthen institutional collaboration with industry partners for internships, apprenticeships, and practical exposure. iv) To ensure timely, accessible, and high-quality delivery of training services across physical, online, and hybrid platforms. v) To maintain transparent engagement processes, including needs assessments, MoU development, training delivery, and reporting. vi) To support continuous monitoring, evaluation, and improvement of training and partnership outcomes.

title: Institutional Communication, Branding, Marketing and Public Relations
supporting_units: ICT Unit, Office of the School Registrar’s Office, Human Resource Directorate, Faculties, PDU, Management Quality: Adherence to Government Communicators Forum, MUBS HR Manual, PRAU Code, UCC Communication Act (2013)
pathway: Information Verification → Communication → Branding → Crisis Management → Publicity → Stakeholder Engagement → Digital Communication Standard Owner: Public Relations Office (PRO) Supporting Units: ICT Unit, Office of the School Registrar’s Office, Human Resource Directorate, Faculties, PDU, Management Quality: Adherence to Government Communicators Forum, MUBS HR Manual, PRAU Code, UCC Communication Act (2013)
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for managing communication, branding, marketing, crisis response, and stakeholder engagement at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that all institutional communication is timely, accurate, compliant, and aligned with institutional and national communication standards. ii) To enhance the visibility, reputation, and promotion of MUBS programmes through effective branding and marketing activities. iii) To strengthen rapid, coordinated, and risk-mitigating crisis communication management. iv) To maintain consistent, accurate, moderated, and engaging content across institutional social media platforms. v) To ensure effective publicity and media coverage of institutional events and activities. vi) To promote strategic, inclusive, and sustainable stakeholder engagement and relationship management. vii) To facilitate timely and accurate dissemination of information through digital and print communication platforms.

matrix_rows.process_text: Identify, log, and classify the operational or reputational crisis (Minute 0) → Convene the core Institutional Crisis Response Team (Within 30 minutes) → Draft an official factual holding statement (Within 1 hour) → Secure executive approval from Management / Principal (Within 2 hours) → Disseminate statement to media houses and targeted stakeholder groups (Within 2–3 hours) → Issue daily verified updates until full resolution of the crisis → Compile and submit a comprehensive final crisis evaluation report to Management (Within 7 days). title: Graduate Tracer Studies, and Institutional Membership Renewal
supporting_units: Office of the School Registrar’s Office, Public Relations Office, ICT Unit, Alumni Office, Finance Quality: Accurate, timely, compliant with NCHE, UOTIA 2001, Government Communicators Forum, PRAU, UCC Act 2013
pathway: Tracer Studies → Membership Renewal → Programme → Reporting Standard Owner: Quality Assurance Directorate (QAD) Supporting Units: Office of the School Registrar’s Office, Public Relations Office, ICT Unit, Alumni Office, Finance Quality: Accurate, timely, compliant with NCHE, UOTIA 2001, Government Communicators Forum, PRAU, UCC Act 2013
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for tracking graduate employability, maintaining institutional memberships, and marketing MUBS academic programmes.
objectives: To conduct annual graduate tracer studies to inform programme review, accreditation processes, and employability enhancement. ii) To ensure timely renewal of institutional memberships with national and international bodies in order to maintain compliance and recognition. iii) To market Makerere University Business School academic programmes accurately, professionally, and in compliance with national communication standards. iv) To provide stakeholders with timely, accurate, and relevant information about institutional programmes and overall standing.

title: Research Archiving, Publishing and Library User Services
supporting_units: Faculties, Graduate School, ICT Unit, Research Directorate, Quality Assurance Directorate Quality: Peer-reviewed, ethical, metadata-compliant, accessible, aligned with international repository standards
pathway: Research Submission → Verification → Metadata Creation → Archiving → Access Provision → User Services → Resource Circulation Standard Owner: School Library Supporting Units: Faculties, Graduate School, ICT Unit, Research Directorate, Quality Assurance Directorate Quality: Peer-reviewed, ethical, metadata-compliant, accessible, aligned with international repository standards
purpose: This standard defines the minimum expectations, processes, and quality benchmarks for managing research outputs, archiving scholarly work, and providing high-quality library services at MUBS.
objectives: To ensure that all dissertations, theses, datasets, and publications are archived in accordance with international metadata standards and open science requirements. ii) To verify that research outputs undergo plagiarism screening, ethical compliance assessment, and metadata validation prior to publication. iii) To provide library users with access to current, relevant, peer-reviewed, and credible academic resources. iv) To strengthen teaching, learning, research, and innovation through timely library processing services, user sensitization, and continuous support. v) To enhance the efficient and transparent management of resource circulation, renewals, returns, and penalties within the library system.

% of research outputs compliant with plagiarism, ethical, and metadata standards before publication.
